title: Espeletiopsis × bogotensis
intro: Espeletiopsis × bogotensis is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to high-altitude regions in South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis and reduce oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Espeletiopsis × bogotensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, and is also employed in the production of herbal supplements and phytomedicines.

preparations: Espeletiopsis × bogotensis is a plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Colombia, where herbal preparations are made from its leaves to treat various ailments. The leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. In local herbal medicine, they are used to treat conditions such as colds, fever, and digestive issues. Preparations may include infusions, tinctures, or salves made from the leaves, which are often combined with other herbs for added therapeutic effects.
